results.title: Class Action: Spindrift Beverages Falsely Advertised as Containing Only Two ‘Simple’ Ingredients [UPDATE]
results.text:
[UPDATE] Class Action: Spindrift Beverages Falsely Advertised as Containing Only Two Ingredients
*in*[Newswire](https://classaction.org/news)Published on January 10, 2023
# Class Action: Spindrift Beverages Falsely Advertised as Containing Only Two ‘Simple’ Ingredients [UPDATE]
by[Kelly Mehorter](https://classaction.org/about-us/kelly-mehorter)
Last Updated on October 15, 2024
**Share[View Comments](#comments)
### Pantoni, et al. v. Spindrift Beverage Co. Inc
Filed: January 3, 2023◆[§ 3:23-cv-00014](#embedded-document)
[•Read Complaint](#embedded-document)
A class action alleges that certain Spindrift beverages are falsely labeled as containing only carbonated water and fruit or alcoholic carbonated water and fruit since they have a third, artificial ingredient.
Defendant(s)
[Spindrift Beverage Co. Inc](https://classaction.org/news/category/spindrift-beverage-co-inc)
Law(s)
[California Business and Professions Code](https://classaction.org/news/category/california-business-and-professions-code)[California Unfair Competition Law](https://classaction.org/news/category/california-unfair-competition-law)[California Consumers Legal Remedies Act](https://classaction.org/news/category/california-consumers-legal-remedies-act)
State(s)
California
Categories
[Food](https://classaction.org/news/category/food)[False Advertising](https://classaction.org/news/category/false-advertising)[Fraud](https://classaction.org/news/category/fraud)
## October 10, 2024 –Spindrift Lawsuit Update: Class Action Suit Dismissed
The class action lawsuit against Spindrift detailed on this page was[voluntarily dismissed](https://www.classaction.org/blog/why-do-class-action-lawsuits-get-dismissed)without prejudice by the plaintiff on March 6, 2023.
The one-page[notice of voluntary dismissal](https://www.classaction.org/media/pantoni-v-spindrift-beverage-company-notice-voluntary-dismissal.pdf)does not state a reason as to why the plaintiff opted to drop the lawsuit.
***Want to stay in the loop on class actions that matter to you? Sign up for***[***ClassAction.org’s free weekly newsletter***](https://www.classaction.org/newsletter-sign-up)***.***
**New to ClassAction.org? Read our Newswire Disclaimer
A proposed class action alleges that certain Spindrift-brand sparking water and spiked sparkling water beverages are falsely labeled as containing only carbonated water and fruit or alcoholic carbonated water and fruit since they have a third, artificial ingredient.
![](https://classaction.org/media/spindrift-sparkling-water.jpg)
According to the 65-page case, the front label of the non-alcoholic and alcoholic drinks made by Spindrift represents to consumers that the products contain only “sparkling water” or “spiked sparkling water” and “real squeezed fruit,” promising, “yup, that’s it.” Contrary to these representations, certain flavors of each product are made with citric acid, a synthetically manufactured artificial flavoring agent and preservative that is not derived from fruits, c

results.title: 10 Spindrift Sparkling Water Flavors, Ranked Worst To Best
results.text:
10 Spindrift Sparkling Water Flavors, Ranked Worst To Best

# 10 Spindrift Sparkling Water Flavors, Ranked Worst To Best

By Samantha Maxwell May 11, 2025 10:15 am EST

Samantha Maxwell/Tasting Table

There are seemingly countless canned sparkling water brands out there, all promising the most delicious, no-sugar-added, bubbly beverage. Some of those brands are ideal when you're looking for a lighter, milder seltzer. But for those who want a more flavorful sparkling water without all the extra sugar, Spindrift may just be the best in the business. Instead of the typical added flavors you'll find in drinks from a lot of brands, Spindrift actually uses real juice (or tea) in its drinks, offering a more flavorful, fruit-forward experience than many sparkling water brands have to offer.

Whether you've tried Spindrift before or are interested in trying out the trendy drink for the first time, you may be wondering which flavors are worth trying and which you can leave on the shelf. Although everyone has different tastes when it comes to beverages, hopefully, this ranking, which works its way from worst to best, can better help you choose which Spindrift flavor you want to snag the next time you're at the store. You might just discover your next favorite carbonated water or soda replacement in the process.

## 10. Peach strawberry

Samantha Maxwell/Tasting Table

I love both peach and strawberry, so I was fully expecting to like the peach strawberry flavor from Spindrift. After one singular sip, though, I realized how wrong I was. If you ask me, this is the absolute worst Spindrift flavor of any in this lineup. Although it sounds like a decent flavor combo, something about the peach and strawberry juice coming together didn't work. The notes of peach tasted strong and perhaps too ripe, like the peach they used had been sitting in the sun for far too long and had turned soft. On the other hand, the strawberry flavor didn't seem ripe enough — instead of contributing the expected sweetness to the drink, it almost seemed to add a touch of tartness alongside that iconic fruity flavor.

If you're going to get peach strawberry Spindrift, your best bet is to make sure it's chilled before you drink it. That will cut down on the strange, too-ripe flavor that you're more likely to encounter when the beverage is warm. But considering that there are so many other delicious Spindrift flavors you could choose from, I suggest skipping this one entirely.

## 9. Mango orange

Samantha Maxwell/Tasting Table

Wondering why you don't see more mango juice in your local grocery store? Probably because it doesn't taste that great when it's not super fresh. That's certainly the case when it comes to Spindrift's mango orange flavor. Admittedly, it's a big step up from the peach strawberry flavor, but it's still one of my least favorites of the bunch. The mango stands out in this drink, with a distinct, ultra-sweet, tropical flavor that doesn't taste great, especially when the c

results.title: All 19 Spindrift Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Taste Test Guide
results.author: RON SILBERSTEIN
results.text:
All 19 Spindrift Flavors Ranked (April 2026) Taste Test Guide

Spindrift flavors ranked from worst to best – that is exactly what I set out to discover after noticing this sparkling water brand everywhere from grocery stores to office refrigerators. Unlike typical sparkling waters that use natural flavors (a vague term that often means artificial), Spindrift stands apart by using real fruit juice and fruit purees in every can.

Our team spent three weeks testing 19 different Spindrift varieties. We tasted them chilled, at room temperature, and even as cocktail mixers. Some flavors blew us away with their authenticity. Others missed the mark entirely. I will walk you through every single flavor, explain why each ranks where it does, and help you avoid wasting money on the duds.

By the end of this guide, you will know exactly which Spindrift flavors deserve space in your refrigerator and which ones you should leave on the shelf.

Table of Contents

Toggle

## Our Taste Testing Methodology

Before diving into the rankings, let me explain how we evaluated each flavor. All testing occurred in our office kitchen over three weeks in March 2026. We purchased every flavor available at three major retailers to ensure we did not miss any varieties.

Each flavor underwent testing at two temperatures: straight from the refrigerator at 38 degrees Fahrenheit, and after sitting out for 30 minutes to simulate a beach or poolside scenario. We evaluated five criteria for every can: flavor accuracy (does it taste like the actual fruit), sweetness level, carbonation intensity, refreshment factor, and overall drinkability. Multiple team members scored each flavor independently, and we averaged the results to create these rankings.

One important note: Spindrift contains real juice, which means the flavors taste different from artificially-flavored sparkling waters like LaCroix. Some people love this intensity. Others find it overwhelming. Keep your personal preferences in mind as you read these rankings.

## The Bottom Tier: Flavors to Skip

Let us start with the flavors that disappointed us. These ranked at the bottom of our taste test for various reasons – some tasted artificial despite the real juice claim, others had strange aftertastes, and a few simply lacked the refreshment factor we want from sparkling water.

### 19. Nojito

Nojito attempts to capture the essence of a mojito without the alcohol. The concept sounds appealing, but the execution falls flat. The mint flavor dominates aggressively, overwhelming any lime notes present. Several testers described the aftertaste as reminiscent of chewing gum left in a hot car.

The real problem with Nojito is the imbalance. Real mojitos achieve harmony between mint, lime, and sweetness. This canned version tips too far toward the herbal mint side, creating something that feels more like a mouthwash than a refreshing beverage. Skip this one unless you absolutely love intense mint flavors.

### 18. Cosmopolitan

The Co

results.title: Spindrift Sparkling Water allegedly not made with all natural ingredients |  | legalnewsline.com
results.author: Marian Johns
results.text:
Spindrift Sparkling Water allegedly not made with all natural ingredients | | legalnewsline.com

You have permission to edit this article.

Edit

Share This

- State AGs & Supreme Courts

SAN FRANCISCO (Legal Newsline) — A lawsuit alleges the maker of Spindrift Sparkling Water drinks fraudulently label and market its product as having only natural ingredients.

Madeline Pantoni, individually and on behalf of all others similarly situated, filed a complaint Jan. 3 in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California against Spindrift Beverage Company Inc., alleging violation of the California Unfair Competition Law and other claims.

Pantoni claims in her class action that the defendants "falsely and misleadingly" label its various flavors of Spindrift Sparkling Water products and Spiked Sparkling Water as having only two ingredients - sparking water and real fruit. She alleges that the drinks contain citric acid, a synthetic artificial flavoring agent.

Pantoni claims the defendant is taking advantage of "consumers' desire, perceived value and willingness to pay more" for "clean products." She further claims the defendant uses cheaper and easier-to-produce ingredients and falsely markets its Sprindrift Sparkling Water products at the expense of consumers and to maintain an unfair competitive advantage.

Pantoni alleges the defendant not only makes misleading and false statements on the drinks' labeling but also through its website and social media platforms.

Pantoni and the class seek monetary relief, interest, trial by jury and all other just relief. They are represented by Ryan Clarkson, Katherine Bruce, Kelsey Elling and Olivia Treister of The Clarkson Law Firm PC in Malibu, California.

U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California case number 3:23-CV-00014SK

results.title: The Worst Spindrift Sparkling Water Flavor, According To Our Taste Test
results.text:
The Worst Spindrift Sparkling Water Flavor, According To Our Taste Test

Advertisement

Advertisement

## Don't Miss:

- Dogsledding ethics in Norway
- Play Crushable by Candy Crush
- AI meal planning tips
- Top home gym picks under $100
- Boy kibble: easy meal trend
- Pepper color versus flavor
- 10-step skincare, less is more
- Keep store basil alive
- Umami ice cream trend
- Top cycling states 2026
- Library card unlocks free perks

spindrift drinks on shelf - PJ McDonnell/Shutterstock

Key takeaways

- Spindrift's peach strawberry flavor ranks at the bottom due to overwhelming sweetness and conflicting fruit notes.
- The use of real peach puree in Spindrift's peach strawberry flavor contributes to its overly ripe and sickeningly sweet taste.
- Compared to other Spindrift flavors like Island Punch and Poppi's strawberry lemon soda, the peach strawberry flavor falls short in achieving a balanced and refreshing taste.

See more

Spindrift has taken the internet by storm lately. In a sea of pretty, mid-tasting sparkling waters, it stands out with bold, brash flavors that we love to see. But as with any brand, Spindrift has some drinks that knock it out of the park and some that leave us underwhelmed. In our list of 10 Spindrift sparkling water flavors, ranked worst to best, the peach strawberry flavor landed at the bottom of the list. With overly saccharine peach notes and nearly sour strawberry, you're getting a confusing flavor experience that will leave you wishing you'd grabbed another can.

What about this flavor makes it a dud? To us, the peach notes are almost too ripe. With a modest ingredients list of sparkling water, peach puree, strawberry puree, citric acid, and sugar, that extreme sweetness overwhelms the palate. It also makes it difficult to pick up on the strawberry, which tastes under-ripe by comparison, bringing a tartness that might have complemented the peach's sweetness well, but just tastes like a flavor cacophony. While it's not the worst thing ever, it's nowhere near Spindrift's best offering.

Read more: 11 Coffee Mate Liquid Creamers, Ranked

## How Spindrift's Peach Strawberry Stacks Up To Other Flavors

peach strawberry spindrift in jar - Samantha Maxwell/Tasting Table

We've talked about how the worst Ramune soda tastes disappointingly artificial, and that flavor was peach. Artificial peach flavors can just be misses for some people. Since Spindrift uses peach puree rather than artificial flavoring, it should presumably lack some of that strange, artificial taste that haters of peach candies and soju reel at. But tragically, using real peach potentially adds to the problem of it tasting overly ripe and sickeningly sweet. Compare that to our top-ranked Spindrift flavor, Island Punch, which has the perfect balance of tropical fruit for a refreshing, not-too-sweet sipping experience, and you'll see exactly why peach strawberry sunk so low.

Advertisement

results.title: The Worst Spindrift Soda: Avoid Buying This Flavor
results.text:
The Worst Spindrift Soda: Avoid Buying This Flavor

# The Spindrift Soda Flavor You Should Stay Far Away From

By Ashlen Wilder July 7, 2025 6:35 am EST

New Africa/Shutterstock

In a vast sea of sparkling water options, Spindrift stands out for its use of fruit juices rather than artificial flavorings. It offers classic citrus flavors such as lime, lemon, and grapefruit, but also brings some more creative offerings to the table, such as Island Punch and Tropical Lemonade. So when the company announced it was launching sodas made from fruit juice and no added sugars, we were naturally excited to give them a try. To our dismay, these better-for-you sodas didn't quite hit the mark, and there's one flavor in particular you might want to steer clear of.

In Chowhound's tasting of Spindrift's five soda flavors, the Concord Freeze came in dead last. The soda is flavored with Concord grape juice, lemon juice, and apple juice, but according to our taster, the apple juice was the more forward scent of this soda. Because there are no concentrated juices or artificial flavorings present, the grape flavor was quite weak. This makes a lot of sense, as grapes naturally don't have a super strong flavor, but conventional grape sodas and candy often used a synthetic and more potent version of it. So if you're expecting the candy-like, in-your-face, fake grape flavor from Spindrift's Concord Freeze soda, you're bound to be disappointed.

The flavor wasn't the only letdown — the level of sweetness and lack of carbonation were also unimpressive. Even with two types of sweet juice, grape and apple, this beverage didn't have the sweetness level expected in a soda. Nor did it contain the bubbles of a soda, but rather a light effervescence.

## Avoid Spindrift's Concord Freeze soda at all costs

Sara Klimek/Chowhound

It's best not to expect a traditional soda experience when you pick up a can of Spindrift's Concord Freeze. This is especially true if you are accustomed to conventional brands of soda like Sunkist or Crush, which have an almost overwhelming fake grape flavor. That said, grape soda, an iconic drink in the South, is rather divisive — there are those who love it and those who wouldn't touch it with a 10-foot pole. Ironically, if you typically don't like grape soda, this beverage might actually work for you.

For health-conscious eaters and drinkers, this "soda" might be the perfect beverage for an afternoon pick-me-up. If you think of it more as a lightly sparkling juice, then there is a lower chance of it being a letdown. We do love the fact that there are no added sugars and each can contains only 7 grams of natural fruit sugars — this is super impressive considering a 12-ounce can of Sunkist grape soda has a shocking 45 grams of added sugar per can.

Overall, the Spindrift sodas aren't the company's best product, and you might want to just stick with its sparkling water line — which actually experienced some controversy in 2023. If you do try the new line

# I Tried the Spindrift Soda, and It’s Not What I Was Expecting

Gael Cooper For Taste Of Home

By Gael Fashingbauer Cooper

Taste of Home's Editorial Process

Published on Mar. 05, 2025

Save Saved Saving

I love Spindrift's fabulous sparkling water, but this soda fell short.

 

I’m a huge fan of soda. Look in my fridge right now, and you’ll find plenty of the new Orange Cream Coke and Dr Pepper Blackberry. I also keep a case of Pepsi Zero on hand at all times.

You might be surprised to learn that soda shares space on the shelf with Spindrift sparkling water. Spindrift is the only flavored sparkling water we buy these days. It’s replaced La Croix in our shopping cart, as we prefer Spindrift’s flavors (especially grapefruit) and love that it uses actual fruit.

I was thrilled when Spindrift released a line of soda that, like its water, features real fruit. The line has five flavors: Shirley Temple, Concord Freeze, Strawberry Shortcake, Ginger Ale and Orange Cream Float. Every one of them sounds scrumptious!

## Spindrift Soda Review

Gael Cooper For Taste Of Home

It didn’t take long for my excitement to fizzle. The second I started my taste test, I knew something was up.

### Strawberry Shortcake

The very first Spindrift soda I tried, Strawberry Shortcake, sounded like it would meld fruity strawberry and sweet vanilla. It even poured out in a pretty pink color. But then, I tasted it. My first thought was, is this vinegar? There’s an odd, kombucha-like tang to the drink. I understand Spindrift soda leans heavy o

results.title: Spindrift Discontinues Popular Hard Seltzer and Fans Are Upset - Parade
results.author: Alex Gurley
results.text:
Spindrift Discontinues Popular Hard Seltzer and Fans Are Upset - Parade

America's Favorite Small Towns 2026

Vote Now & Be Entered to Win a Trip Valued at $1500

# Beverage Company Discontinues Popular Hard Seltzer and Fans Are Upset

The wildly popular beverage brand is facing backlash for discontinuing their hard seltzer.

Aug 19, 2025 5:27 PM EDT

By Alex Gurley

Entertainment Writer, Parade

0

Add us on

Photo by Kevin Carter/Getty Images

Spindrift fans are facing some disappointing news after the company announced that they’re discontinuing a fan-favorite product.

Early this week, the wildly popular sparkling water brand shared an update that they would no longer be selling Spiked Spindrift, the alcoholic version of their sparkling water. The hard seltzer has been on shelves since 2021 but in a statement, Spindrift shared that now that many customers are changing their relationship with alcohol, it would no longer be their focus.

“After four years, we’ve decided to discontinue our Spindrift Spiked product line to focus on what we do best: bringing real ingredients to sparkling water and innovating new non-alcoholic categories with real squeezed fruit,” the brand shared in a social media statemen t.

They continued, “It has been an incredible run, and we’re proud of all we accomplished with Spiked, but as more consumers reassess their relationship with alcohol, we’re excited to double down on our core Spindrift products.”

😋😋 SIGN UP to get delicious recipes, handy kitchen hacks & more in our daily Pop Kitchen newsletter🍳🍔

On their website, the brand explained their decision to move away from alcoholic beverages, sharing that they wanted to honor their “long-time commitment to our core sparkling water business” and continue to do what they do best.

They also noted that as Spindrift Spiked was the company’s first foray into the wold of alcoholic beverages, they quickly realized that the “business demands substantial resources and time due to the unique route to market, dedicated manufacturing requirements, and necessary regulatory compliance.” On top of that, over the past several years, there has also been a “declining hard seltzer demand.”

“Making the decision to move on from a beloved product line is never easy. We are incredibly proud of the impact that our team was able to achieve in the marketplace, and this is a difficult decision, but the right one to better serve the needs of our customers, supporting the tremendous momentum of the current business and strong consumer trends towards non-alc beverages,” CEO Dave Burwick shared.

Unfortunately, the announcement didn’t sit well with many fans of the brand, who saw it as one of the best hard seltzers on the market.

“Why are you ruining my life??? This is the only hard seltzer that doesn’t taste artificial or give me a headache,” one fan wrote on Instagram, with another adding, “This is so incredibly disappointing, the only seltzer on the market without trash ingredients.”

So
